summary: In 1997, I was living in Jalisco, Mexico with my family in a small house. My parents, Lorena and Jesse, both lived there before deciding to move to California. My mom was very religious and attended church every Sunday in Jalisco, where my parents met. It was in Jalisco that my mom was pregnant with my twin brothers before they made the difficult decision to cross the border.

California was where my dad's family lived, and it was the place my parents wanted to move to so my brothers could be considered legal. My dad was actually born in California, even though he lived in Jalisco before. Crossing into California was risky, especially for my mom who had no papers, but she bravely hid under gallons of gas in a truck to get through the border. After arriving in California, my parents worked hard to provide better opportunities for us, and my mom even fought cancer while pregnant with my older brother there.
